item.description: Few and far between
item.dc:creator: GullahGullahIslander

item.title: Mentally healthy dump
item.description:
I'm adding this one because I remember at some point somebody said they really liked this very much and I told myself I would put it in a dump and tag them. I can't remember who it was though so I'm just going to keep posting it until they see it.
Sorry for the quality on this one. The website that originally posted it doesn't exist anymore so I can't seem to find a better one.
Sorry for the shorter dump, I've been getting so many error messages over the weeks that I've tried to upload, I would say probably 90% of my attempted dumps get rejected and well, it brings my mood down pretty hard and I often give up. I decided to do shorter dumps but I'll post two in the same day, that way if it's one image bringing down the whole bunch, it's only at most 25 I need to go through instead of 50.
item.dc:creator: iamthecomet

item.title: Comet relief: broadcasting from a dead satellite- check your Wi-Fi
item.dc:creator: iamthecomet

item.title: German state says no to Microsoft
item.description: Usually governmental institutions don’t change things lightly. They rely on certain structures and processes. In this case a state started to switch 60.000 workplaces to Linux. I read a German article from today and I added a link to an English article. It is a couple days older and reports a lower number. https://www.zdnet.com/article/german-state-ditches-microsoft-for-linux-and-libreoffice/ If this works more states will follow. The reasons are independence, security and costs. Edit: I found a more up to date article. My apologies: https://licenseware.io/from-microsoft-to-open-source-how-one-german-state-is-rewriting-the-rules-of-public-sector-it/
item.dc:creator: makeamericasmartagain9000

item.title: Nicknames for coworkers
item.dc:creator: AdventurousDonut480

item.title: Banana
item.description:
I guess it is banana day. so i will post for trophy. falling off posting and pretty much everything online aside from gaming cause its too much. barely keeping my stuff irl together because well *gestures vaguely at everything*. so here are the memes that came up when i searched my memes for banana. enjoy.
is. is there an option for bone in bananas?
mhmm. going to be doing a grocery order thing soon and it is such a process.
someone fixed the above one for others preferences.
please? only one person i wanna do dis with tho. you know who you are. usually put copypasta stuff at the end of my posts and dont feel like it. dunno if i will post anymore soon. will be around in my discord for gaming. been sucked into learning satisfactory and do have a nice lil server going for that. probably helldivers 2 on friday night like usual.
item.dc:creator: PotatoCheesMonk

item.title: MTG Tases constituents for speaking
item.description:
MTG hires police to assault and tase her constituents for simply speaking against her policies. There was no violence displayed by any constituent. https://imgur.com/gallery/mtg-police-assault-town-hall-anti-trans-legislation-zXwIUAD
MTG is a public servant, however, she seems to think that constituents should be assaulted and tased when disagreeing with her policies. https://imgur.com/gallery/mtg-police-assault-town-hall-anti-trans-legislation-zXwIUAD
item.dc:creator: AnonymousMockingjay

item.title: Tiny li'l Midweek Dumpy Doo
item.description:
Better than George R. R. Martin
Pfft. She once hovered in the air dancing to "Jump in the line" while a ghost football team danced on the stairs behind her.
Do you want The Last of Us? Because this is how you get The Last of Us.
At least SOMETHING productive came out of it.
It's a vicious cycle.
It's smaller on the outside
Clever girl
"I'm here with local octopus Otto Tentacleson, who was just released on bail from assaulting a starfish, who wished to remain anonymous. Otto, what are your thoughts?"
Ctrl+S FTW
item.dc:creator: ddubya42

item.title: Sugar the rat helping to train some younger rats to play ratsketball!
item.description: This was Sugar (albino) helping to train Olive and Donut ratsketball - rats like to follow other rats so having a helper rat who knows the ropes help to train new tricks can be super helpful! . Want help with training your rats tricks? I have a rat training book available here! https://www.etsy.com/listing/1605480026/the-ultimate-guide-to-rat-training
item.dc:creator: ShadowtheRat

item.dc:creator: tolson9

item.title: We Made It to the World Robotics Championship — From Libya to Houston, USA!
item.description:
We’re proud to announce that four exceptional LYBOTICS teams from Libya are representing our nation at the World Robotics Championship 2025, taking place from April 16–19 in Houston, Texas.
Out of over 100 local robotics teams who competed in the LYBOTICS Regional Robotics Championship, these four teams rose to the top: ✨ LYBOTICS Wizards Team ? LYBOTICS SuperNova Team ?️ LYBOTICS IronSide Team ⚙️ LYBOTICS IronFist Team These young STEM leaders are showcasing their talent, teamwork, and innovation on a global stage, proving that Libyan youth can compete with the best in the world.
A Special Thanks to the U.S. Embassy in Libya During their visa journey, the teams had the unique opportunity to meet with U.S. Chargé d’Affaires Jeremy Brent, who showed great enthusiasm and support for our mission. We extend our sincere thanks to the U.S. Embassy in Libya for facilitating this life-changing experience for our students.
Why It Matters Libya’s youth are full of potential, but access to quality STEM education remains limited in many regions. LYBOTICS is working tirelessly to close this gap—by building a national robotics ecosystem, empowering young minds, and fostering peace through innovation.
? Now is the time to support STEM education in Libya. Help us inspire, educate, and empower the next generation of engineers, innovators, and changemakers. ? Contact us to collaborate, donate, or become a partner at info@LYBOTICS.com or at Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/Lybotics ? Visit www.LYBOTICS.com to learn more. #STEMEducation #Robotics #Libya #LYBOTICS #WorldRoboticsChampionship #YouthInnovation #SupportSTEM #MakeLibyaShine #TechForGood #FirstChampionship2025
item.dc:creator: LYBOTICS

item.title: It's been quite a day.
item.description: Seven years ago he suffered a fairly serious head injury. He was hospitalised but somehow the doctors didn't know he was on blood thinnners so the medication they gave him caused catastrophic brain bleeds so he was put into a coma to recover. After a couple of months the doctors told us it was time to prepare to pull the plug but then he rallied and emerged from the coma. He went to rehab and re-learned to walk. The rehabilitation hospital were pressuring us to take him home but home wasn't remotely suitable for someone on a walking frame. We did our best at our expense to adapt the house but we were waiting for a grant to get some rails put around the house and get a shower he could use. Eventually they threatened to just straight up discharge him unless we tried to take him home so we took him for a weekend and he ended up falling and cracking his head on the floor tiles so back to hospital and rehab again. From there he ended up in a dementia ward because the injury caused symptoms close enough to dementia symptoms. But he was doing alright. He kind of hated it but he made the best of it. Three years later my mother started to exhibit the signs of early onset dementia that he could have told us about had he not suffered repeated head injuries. The stress of all this accelerated it. She was told she'd have six months before she needed to enter a home but she made it to well over a year. She passed a year and a half ago, gasping for breath, surrounded by family after accidentally aspirating some food. Dad made it to her funeral. It was probably the most lucid and aware I'd seen him in a few years so it straight up broke my heart that he had to be so at the funeral of the woman he'd loved and cherished for almost fifty years. For a month or two after, when we visited he was brighter and sharper than he'd been for years before but slowly that began to fade. Every visit for the last fourteen months he was either asleep in bed or asleep in a chair. Sometimes his eyes we open, sometimes not. It made little difference. He was put on palliative care fo the third time last weekend. On Monday afternoon we were told we should come in for a visit. He had stopped eating or drinking properly a week before and he had a chest infection. They'd stopped antibiotics for it because he wouldn't swallow and they knew it was time. He had good colour in his cheeks, an oxygen mask on his face, an O2 stat of 90 but otherwise doing well. At 7:50 this morning I got a call to say he had passed and I was glad. The line between care and torture is a fine one sometimes. He wasn't a great dad but he was a phenomenal grandad. He expressed regret (to my wife, never to me) that he had spent so much time working instead of hanging out with us and that he had been a harsh disciplinarian. With my own kids he was everything I wished he could have been with me and I don't begrudge any of them that experience. They got an amazing grandad and he got to be the person he wanted and needed to be. So he's gone, and I'm glad. His suffering is over. Maybe my whole atheism thing is wrong and he gets to go be with his wife and the family that came before him and now he waits for me and mine or maybe I'm right and his suffering is over his matter rejoins the cosmos in some small way. Regardless, there were better dads, there were worse dads but my dad tried his best and in the end, maybe that's all that matters. (the obligatory edit: There are too many replies for me to respond to and we're obviously a bit on the busy side right now but I want you all to know I read and appreciated every single one of them. You're a lovely bunch Imgur.)
item.dc:creator: diemajorthrilldie
